Compare Period Report: Capture vs. Replay

Collapse all sections Expand all sections

This report compares the performance of a workload replay against the performance of the original captured system. Throughout the report "Capture" refers to the original captured system, while "Replay" refers to the replayed workload. The most reliable experiment would compare two replays. The first replay would try to mimic the captured system as much as possible without any system changes. The second replay would be similar to the first while applying a single change as the test variable. (Test systems are almost always an approximation of production. The idea in comparing two replays is to isolate the change we want to apply and thus assess the effect of such a change on a system similar to production.)

(+) (-) General Information

This section describes the experimental setup. Check it carefully to verify that the intended experiment was performed.

(+) (-) Data Sources

Capture Replay
Capture/Replay name SH_load_capture927931 sh_upgradetest_02
Capture/Replay ID 41 34
Data Directory Name EM_REPLAY_TASK_10_1 EM_REPLAY_TASK_10_1
Directory Path /home/opc/replay02 /home/opc/replay02
PL/SQL Mode EXTENDED TOP_LEVEL
Encryption Algorithm

(+) (-) Information About Databases

Capture Replay
Database Name SDB2 DB26
Original Database ID 3665490558 914873574
Version 19.0.0.0.0 23.0.0.0.0
Platform Linux x86 64-bit Linux x86 64-bit
Is It RAC? NO NO
Number of Instances 1 1

(+) (-) Information About AWR and Time Periods

Capture Replay
Start Time 2026-03-27 19:17:22 2026-03-30 17:03:53
End Time 2026-03-27 19:27:25 2026-03-30 17:11:40
Duration 10.05 minutes 7.78 minutes
AWR Database ID 905545248 914873574
AWR Start Snapshot ID 555 47
AWR End Snapshot ID 556 48

(+) (-) Changes to Important Parameters

Capture Replay
compatible 19.0.0 23.6.0

(+) (-) Changes to Optimizer-Relevant Parameters

Capture Replay
pga_aggregate_target 1.53 G 1.52 G
optimizer_features_enable 19.1.0 23.1.0

(+) (-) Changes to Memory Configuration Parameters

Capture Replay
sga_max_size 4.61 G 4.56 G
pga_aggregate_target 1.53 G 1.52 G

(+) (-) Changes to Underscore Parameters

Capture Replay
_instance_recovery_bloom_filter_size NULL 1048576

(+) (-) Instances of the Capture Database

instance number instance name host name number of CPU cores number of CPU sockets physical memory instance type
1 sdb2 sb01 2 1 15.33 G RDBMS

(+) (-) Instances of the Replay Database

instance number instance name host name number of CPU cores number of CPU sockets physical memory instance type
1 db26 ol9test 1 1 15.17 G RDBMS

(+) (-) Replay Divergence

This section describes the divergence in replay compared to the captured system. Please look at the full divergence report if this report shows significant divergence. The possible divergence levels are: (NONE) no divergence detected at all (LOW) minimal divergence detected but the performance comparison is most likely still valid (MEDIUM) some non-trivial divergence is detected and the performance comparison is suspect (HIGH) severe divergence detected and the performance comparison is unlikely to be informative.

Divergence Level Percent of Calls That Diverged
Replay Divergence (compared to Capture) LOW 2.79%

(+) (-) Main Performance Statistics

This section does a high-level performance comparison of the two periods. Start by looking for a change in Database Time. If there is no significant change in Database Time, you can assume performance as a whole is similar. You can look for a change in the Database Time pieces that follow (CPU, User I/O, and Cluster) to see how the different ingredients of Database Time changed from one period to the next, either to explain a change in Database Time or to see if some pieces regressed and others improved.

Change in DB time Capture total time Replay total time Capture % of DB time Replay % of DB time
Database Time 347.12% 7572.39 seconds 33857.78 seconds 100 100
CPU Time 7.37% 808.2 seconds 867.75 seconds 10.67 2.56
User I/O Wait Time N/A 0 seconds 0 seconds 0 0

(+) (-) Top SQL/Call

(+) (-) Top SQL by Change in DB Time

This section compares the performance change of individual SQL statements from one period to the next. SQL statements are identified by their force matching signature to account for literal usage. They are ordered by the total change in DB Time, as the most relevant changes are those that impact total throughput the most. Any SQL tuning you do should begin with the statement that regressed by the most DB Time.

(+) (-) Top Call by Change in DB Time

This section compares the performance change of individual database calls from one period to the next. A call is identified by File ID and Call Counter. They are ordered by the change in DB Time, as the most relevant changes are those that impact total throughput the most. SQL_ID and SQL text are displayed for information purpose about the call. Any SQL tuning you do should begin with the statement that regressed by the most DB Time.

(+) (-) Hardware Usage Comparison

(+) (-) CPU Usage

This section describes general CPU usage on the systems and helps assess if they were CPU bound. The number of CPUs is summed over all instances. CPU usage is averaged over instances. Note that "Oracle Run-queue Load" is for Oracle processes only and usually underestimates the run-queue part.

system CPU Sockets/Cores/Threads Hosts' CPU Usage Oracle Sessions on CPU Oracle Run-queue Load
Capture 1/2/2 86.89% 1.34 active sessions 10.75 active sessions
Replay 1/1/2 99.92% 1.88 active sessions 41.35 active sessions

(+) (-) I/O to Data and Temp Files

This section shows some basic I/O statistics about data and temp files. First look at the single block read time. An I/O bound system will show a high value (much more than 10 miliseconds). The total read/write rates should provide some guidance to determine if high single-block latencies are caused by an excess of requests from the database or poor I/O throughput in general.

Capture Replay
average time of single block read (miliseconds) .78 0
total number of single block reads 18 66
total physical read G 0 G
total physical write G .144 G
read rate M per sec 0M per sec
write rate M per sec .315M per sec

(+) (-) ADDM Comparison

This section provides a comparison of ADDM analyses of the two time periods ordered by the absolute difference in impact. By comparing ADDM results for the two periods, you can see problems that existed in one but not the other, as well as a change in individual finding impacts. If your change is intended to improve database throughput, you should see that improvement reflected in the ADDM findings and impacts.

impact (sec) average active sessions percent active sessions
Large number of database connections

Replay

(+) (-) Common SQL

This section reports the common sql in both the time periods. Note that this only reports sqls with significant db time (not all common sql).

(+) (-) Common SQL By Total DB Time

SQL ID SQL Text Total DB Time(1) Total DB Time(2) DIFF(Total DB Time)
gujdfhgzwqm98
(+) (-) SELECT * FROM (SELECT times.calendar_quarter_desc, customers.cust_first_name, customers.cust_last_name, customers.cust_id, SUM(sales.amount_sold), rank() over(PARTITION BY times.calendar_quarter_desc ORDER BY SUM(amount_sold) DESC) AS rank_within_quarter FROM sales, customers, times WHERE sales.cust_id = customers.cust_id AND times.calendar_quarter_desc = '2000-4' AND times.time_id = sales.time_id GROUP BY customers.cust_id, customers.cust_first_name, customers.cust_last_name, customers.cust_id, times.calendar_quarter_desc) WHERE rank_within_quarter < 16 SELECT * FROM (SELECT times.calendar_quarte [...]

"WRI$_SQLSET_STATISTICS"."PLAN_HASH_VALUE")) LEADING(@"SEL$66860544" "DEFNS"@"SEL$19" "PLNS"@"SEL$19" "STMTS"@"SEL$19" "STAT"@"SEL$19") USE_NL(@"SEL$66860544" "PLNS"@"SEL$19") USE_NL(@"SEL$66860544" "STMTS"@"SEL$19") USE_NL(@"SEL$66860544" "STAT"@"SEL$19") PUSH_SUBQ(@"SEL$470E2127") FULL(@"SEL$470E2127" "X$KZSPR"@"SEL$22") INDEX_RS_ASC(@"SEL$B01C6807" "S"@"SEL$3" ("WRI$_SQLSET_STATEMENTS"."SQLSET_ID" "WRI$_SQLSET_STATEMENTS"."SQL_ID")) INDEX_RS_ASC(@"SEL$B01C6807" "D"@"SEL$3" ("WRI$_SQLSET_DEFINITIONS"."ID")) LEADING(@"SEL$B01C6807" "S"@"SEL$3" "D"@"SEL$3") USE_NL(@"SEL$B01C6807" "D"@"SEL$3") PUSH_SUBQ(@"SEL$A731BD80") FULL(@"SEL$A731BD80" "X$KZSPR"@"SEL$8") FULL(@"SEL$5" "X$MODACT_LENGTH"@"SEL$5") FULL(@"SEL$4" "X$MODACT_LENGTH"@"SEL$4") END_OUTLINE_DATA */ /* CAPTURE SQLSET QUERY */ new.sql_seq, old.plan_hash_value, sqlset_row(new.sql_id,new.force_matching_signature, new.sql_text, new.object_list, new.bind_data, new.parsing_schema_name, new.module, new.action, new.elapsed_time, new.cpu_time, new.buffer_gets, new.disk_reads, new.direct_writes, new.rows_processed, new.fetches, new.executions, new.end_of_fetch_count, new.optimizer_cost, new.optimizer_env, new.priority, new.command_type, new.first_load_time, new.stat_period, new.active_stat_period, new.other, new.plan_hash_value, new.sql_plan, new.bind_list, new.con_dbid, new.last_exec_start_time, new.sharable_mem, new.exact_matching_signature, new.result_cache_executions, new.sql_profile, new.sql_plan_baseline, new.sql_patch, new.avg_hard_parse_time, new.user_io_wait_time, new.io_interconnect_bytes, new.parsing_user_name ) as new_row , old.plan_timestamp, old.binds_captured , new.last_load_time FROM (SELECT s.sql_seq, c.* FROM sys."_ALL_SQLSET_STATEMENTS_ONLY" s, ( SELECT /*+ first_rows(1) */ sql_id, force_matching_signature, sql_text, cast(NULL as SQL_OBJECTS) object_list, bind_data, parsing_schema_name, module, action, elapsed_time, cpu_time, buffer_gets, disk_reads, direct_writes,rows_processed, fetches, executions, end_of_fetch_count, optimizer_cost, optimizer_env,NULL priority, command_type, first_load_time, last_load_time, null stat_period, null active_stat_period, NULL other, plan_hash_value, cast(NULL as SQL_PLAN_TABLE_TYPE) sql_plan, cast (NULL as SQL_BINDS) bind_list, con_dbid , last_exec_start_time, sharable_mem, exact_matching_signature , result_cache_executions, sql_profile, sql_plan_baseline , sql_patch, avg_hard_parse_time , user_io_wait_time, io_interconnect_bytes, parsing_user_name FROM (SELECT sql_id, force_matching_signature, sql_text, parsing_schema_name, bind_data, module, action, elapsed_time, cpu_time, buffer_gets, disk_reads, direct_writes, rows_processed, fetches, executions, end_of_fetch_count, optimizer_cost, optimizer_env, command_type, first_load_time, last_load_time, plan_hash_value, last_active_child_address, loaded_versions, con_dbid, last_exec_start_time, sharable_mem, exact_matching_signature, result_cache_executions, sql_profile, sql_plan_baseline, sql_patch, avg_hard_parse_time, user_io_wait_time, io_interconnect_bytes, parsing_user_name FROM (SELECT vst.avg_hard_parse_time, vs.sql_id, vs.force_matching_signature, vs.plan_hash_value, vs.sql_text, vs.parsing_schema_name, vs.module, vs.action, vs.elapsed_time, vs.cpu_time, vs.buffer_gets, vs.last_active_child_address, vs.first_load_time, vs.last_load_time, vs.disk_reads, vs.direct_writes, vs.rows_processed, vs.fetches, vs.executions, vs.end_of_fetch_count, vs.optimizer_cost, vs.optimizer_env, vs.command_type, vs.loaded_versions, vs.bind_data, vs.last_active_time, vs.con_dbid, vs.last_exec_start_time, vs.sharable_mem, vs.exact_matching_signature, vs.result_cache_executions, vs.sql_profile, vs.sql_plan_baseline, vs.sql_patch, vs.user_io_wait_time, vs.io_interconnect_bytes, vs.parsing_user_name FROM sys.v_$sqlstats vst, (SELECT sql_id, force_matching_signature, NVL(plan_hash_value, 0) plan_hash_value, sql_fulltext as sql_text, parsing_schema_name, module, action, elapsed_time, cpu_time, buffer_gets, last_active_child_address, TO_CHAR(first_load_time, 'YYYY-MM-DD/HH24:MI:SS') first_load_time, last_load_time, disk_reads, direct_writes, rows_processed, fetches, executions, end_of_fetch_count, optimizer_cost, optimizer_env, command_type, loaded_versions, bind_data, last_active_time, con_dbid, TO_CHAR(last_exec_start_time, 'YYYY-MM-DD/HH24:MI:SS') last_exec_start_time, sharable_mem, exact_matching_signature, result_cache_executions, sql_profile, sql_plan_baseline, sql_patch, NULL avg_hard_parse_time, user_io_wait_time, io_interconnect_bytes, sys.dbms_sqltune_util2.user_id_to_name (parsing_user_id, con_id) as parsing_user_name FROM sys.v_$sqlarea_plan_hash s) vs WHERE vst.sql_id = vs.sql_id and vs.last_active_time >= :lat and vst.last_active_time >= :lat and vs.con_dbid = vst.con_dbid) WHERE executions > 0 AND (parsing_schema_name not in ('SYS', 'SYSTEM')) ) S ) c WHERE s.sqlset_id(+) = :stsid AND s.sql_id(+) = c.sql_id AND s.con_dbid(+) = c.con_dbid) new ,sys."_ALL_SQLSET_STATISTICS_ONLY" old WHERE :stsid = old.sqlset_id(+) AND new.sql_seq = old.sql_seq(+) AND new.plan_hash_value = old.plan_hash_value(+) AND ((new.executions >= old.executions) OR old.sql_seq is NULL) SELECT /*+ BEGIN_OUTLINE_DATA IGN [...]

(+) (-) select 'jobs_running' stat_type,count(*) v1, null v2, null v3, null v4 from ( SELECT j.job_name, j.owner, j.state FROM cdb_scheduler_jobs j WHERE j.job_subname IS NULL AND (j.state = 'RUNNING' OR j.state = 'CHAIN_STALLED')) UNION ALL select 'jobs_failed',count(*) v1, null v2, null v3, null v4 from ( SELECT all_runs.JOB_NAME, all_runs.OWNER, all_runs.STATUS FROM CDB_SCHEDULER_JOB_RUN_DETAILS all_runs, (SELECT CON_ID, JOB_NAME, OWNER, MAX(ACTUAL_START_DATE) AS START_DATE FROM CDB_SCHEDULER_JOB_RUN_DETAILS GROUP BY CON_ID, JOB_NAME, OWNER) latest_runs WHERE latest_runs.CON_ID =all_runs.CON_ID AND latest_runs.OWNER=all_runs.OWNER AND latest_runs.JOB_NAME=all_runs.JOB_NAME AND all_runs.ACTUAL_START_DATE= latest_runs.START_DATE AND all_runs.STATUS = 'FAILED') select 'jobs_running' stat_type,count(*) v1, [...]
1.737 12.375 00000007.05600
gju2wwnqsrm22
(+) (-) SELECT channel_desc, calendar_month_desc, countries.country_iso_code, TO_CHAR(SUM(amount_sold), '9,999,999,999') SALES$ FROM sales, customers, times, channels, countries WHERE sales.time_id=times.time_id AND sales.cust_id=customers.cust_id AND sales.channel_id= channels.channel_id AND customers.country_id = countries.country_id AND channels.channel_desc IN ('Internet','Partners') AND times.calendar_month_desc IN ('2016-11','2016-12','2017-01') AND countries.country_iso_code IN ('IE','IN') GROUP BY CUBE(channel_desc, calendar_month_desc, countries.country_iso_code) SELECT channel_desc, calendar_month_desc, cou [...]
